REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT OR STAY THIS ACTION ON GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, IN PART. ROSENFELD, ET AL V. DEUTSCHE BANK AG, CASE NO. CGC-25-622060, IS STAYED DURING THE PENDENCY OF DEUTSCHE BANK AG, NEW YORK BRANCH V. ROSENFELD, INDEX NO. 659459/2024, FILED IN THE STATE OF NEW YORK. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE WILL BE TWO PARALLEL ACTIONS ARISING OUT OF THE SAME LOAN AND RELATED GUARANTIES BETWEEN SUBSTANTIALLY THE SAME PARTIES, WHICH WILL NEEDLESSLY INCREASE THE BURDEN AND EXPENSE ON THE COURTS AND LITIGANTS, AND WHICH COULD LEAD TO CONFLICTING JUDGMENTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: SHEILA PHAM, CSR 13293 (T: 415.274.9977, E: CALENDAR-LA@VERITEXT.COM)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Appearances: Jenny Pelaez, Jennifer Recine, Amy Nemetz of King & Spalding LLP (213.443.4355) for defendant and moving party; Heath Rosenblat and Aaron Lauchheimer of Morrison Cohen LLP (212.735.8600) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OFF CALENDAR to be re-filed when the stay is lifted if appropriate. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR 13293 (T: 415.274.9977, E: calendar-la@veritext.com) (501/CFH)

Amended Notice Of Continued Hearing On Motion To Dismiss Complaint Or Stay This Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens, Or Alternatively, Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT OR STAY THIS ACTION ON GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS

Appearances: Jenny Pelaez, Jennifer Recine, Amy Nemetz of King & Spalding LLP (213.443.4355) for defendant and moving party; Heath Rosenblat and Aaron Lauchheimer of Morrison Cohen LLP (212.735.8600) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED, in part. Rosenfeld, et al v. Deutsche Bank AG, Case No. CGC-25-622060, is stayed during the pendency of Deutsche Bank AG, New York Branch v. Rosenfeld, Index No. 659459/2024, filed in the state of New York. The Court finds that there will be two parallel actions arising out of the same loan and related guaranties between substantially the same parties, which will needlessly increase the burden and expense on the courts and litigants, and which could lead to conflicting judgments. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR 13293 (T: 415.274.9977, E: calendar-la@veritext.com) (501/CFH)

MINI MINUTES:

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Heath Rosenblat, Esq. 212-735-8600 Morrison Cohen LLP for Plaintiff.
Jennifer Recine, Esq. Amy Nemetz, Jenny Pelaez 213-443-4355 for Defendant DEUTSCHE BANK AG, NEW YORK BRANCH MOTION.

Prior to Open Court, at the instruction of the Court, the clerk informs parties the court did not recieve a notice to contest the third motion; If a clarification is needed from the court, parties still need to follow the procedure for contesting a tentative ruling. The court states it will not hear from the parties unless the opposing side appears via Zoom to avoid an ex parte communication. If the opposing side does not appear, the tentative ruling will stand.

Argued in Open Court. Ms. Pelaez cites section 448.10 and argues "simultaneous". The Court shares its comments. Mr. Rosenbalt responds to Defense' arguments. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling on all three motions.

2 - DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK AG, NEW YORK BRANCH MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Plaintiffs Michael Rosenfeld and Pine and Powell Partners, LLC's Motion to Admit Counsel Pro Hac Vice is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Counsel Amy K. Nemetz may appear as counsel pro hac vice for moving parties in this action.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
